supply_chain.dependencies: Substantially migrated primarily all aspects of core information technology systems and customer-facing applications to third-party cloud infrastructure platforms, principally AWS. Reliance on TSYS for consumer and commercial credit card processing services for North American and U.K. portfolios. Reliance on FIS for certain banking systems.
risks: If AWS or other service providers experience system or telecommunication breakdowns, failures, outages, degradation in service, downtime, failure to scale, software bugs, design flaws, cyber-attacks and other security incidents, insider threats, adverse changes to financial condition, bankruptcy, or other adverse conditions, it could have a material adverse effect on business and reputation. Single source vendor risk where vendor is the sole provider of critical services. Transition to alternative third-party service providers or internal solutions may be difficult to implement, may cause significant time and expense and may disrupt or degrade ability to deliver products and services.

regulatory_risks: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) regulations
Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure Act (CIRCIA) requiring reporting of significant cyber incidents to CISA
Anti-Money Laundering Act of 2020 (AML Act) requiring updates to BSA regulatory requirements
Basel III Capital Rules and proposed amendments (Basel III Finalization Proposal)
Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure Act (CIRCIA)
Volcker Rule prohibitions on proprietary trading and certain investments in covered funds
Sanctions compliance (OFAC)
CFPB final rule amending Regulation Z to significantly lower safe harbor amount for past due fees on consumer credit card accounts
Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) and Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR) requirements
Dodd-Frank Act compliance
Bank Secrecy Act (BSA) and USA PATRIOT Act requirements for AML and combating financing of terrorism
Foreign regulatory requirements in U.K. (FCA) and Canada (OSFI, FCAC)
Resolution and recovery planning requirements under Section 165(d) of Dodd-Frank Act
Federal Reserve and OCC consent orders related to cybersecurity and operational risk management
Broker-dealer regulations by SEC and FINRA
CFPB final rule requiring financial institutions to share certain data on consumer financial products and services upon customer request
Consumer lending regulations (Truth in Lending Act, Equal Credit Opportunity Act, Fair Credit Reporting Act, CRA, Servicemembers Civil Relief Act, Military Lending Act)
Privacy and data protection regulations (GLBA, FCRA, CCPA, PIPEDA, EU GDPR, U.K. GDPR)
Bank Secrecy Act (BSA) and Anti-Money Laundering Act (AML Act) compliance
UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) regulations
Pending regulatory changes and enforcement actions
Changes in capital adequacy requirements under Basel III Capital Rules
Merchant litigation against payment card networks and banks regarding interchange fees and antitrust claims
Climate-related financial risk management principles issued by Federal Banking Agencies
Long-term debt requirements and clean holding company requirements (LTD Proposal)
Stress testing requirements (DFAST) and capital plan rule
Community Reinvestment Act (CRA) requirements
Data privacy regulations including GDPR (EU and UK), CCPA (California), and PIPEDA (Canada)
OFAC and Department of State sanctions programs
Basel III Finalization Proposal requiring new Expanded Risk-Based Approach for calculating risk-weighted assets
FDIC deposit insurance assessments and special assessments
FDIC special assessment related to systemic risk determination
Potential regulation of AI technologies at federal and state levels
Prompt Corrective Action (PCA) capital regulations
Long-Term Debt and Clean Holding Company Proposal (LTD Proposal) requiring minimum outstanding eligible long-term debt
Federal Reserve's proposed amendments to Regulation II to lower cap on debit interchange fees

Increased regulatory scrutiny on risk management, compliance with AML and sanctions laws, privacy, data protection and data security, fair lending, unfair or deceptive practices, consumer protection, and innovative activities such as AI
Truth in Lending Act (TILA) and Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) compliance
Interchange fee restrictions and legislation (federal and state level, including Illinois law prohibiting interchange fees on state taxes and gratuities)
Derivatives regulations under Title VII of Dodd-Frank Act (swap dealer registration with CFTC)
State-level privacy, data protection and data security laws (e.g., California Consumer Privacy Act)
Stress Capital Buffer Rule requirements
Canada's Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI) regulations
Interchange fee regulations and litigation
FDIC Orderly Liquidation Authority

sanction_risks: Sanctions imposed by U.S. and other countries related to war between Ukraine and Russia
Increased geopolitical tensions between U.S. and China
Compliance with sanctions laws and regulations administered by U.S. Treasury Department's OFAC and Department of State
Sanctions imposed by U.S. and other countries related to conflict in the Middle East
